_meta.description: HKDSE grade-to-point conversion scales used by JUPAS universities. From 2025 entry, ALL JUPAS institutions use the enhanced 8.5-point scale for calculating admissions scores. The standard 7pt scale remains used by some institutions for reporting (e.g. HKBU, Lingnan, EdUHK, HKMU) and by CUHK/HKU Medicine.
sources: JUPAS 2025 Admissions Scores PDF (all 9 participating institutions)
JUPAS 2024 Admissions Scores PDF (all 9 participating institutions)

jupas_2025_enhanced.description: Enhanced 8.5-point scale adopted by ALL JUPAS participating institutions from 2025 entry. This is the official unified scale for JUPAS admissions scoring. Used by CityU, PolyU, HKUST, and most HKU/CUHK programmes for score calculation.

category_c_2025.description: Category C Other Language subjects conversion for 2025+ entry

standard_7pt.description: Standard 7-point scale. Still used by HKBU, Lingnan, EdUHK, HKMU, HSUHK for reporting admission scores. Also used by CUHK Medicine (JS4501/JS4502). Note: from 2025, some institutions that report using this scale may still internally use enhanced scoring.

hku_enhanced.description: Alias for jupas_2025_enhanced. HKU was an early adopter of this scale. From 2025, this is the same as the unified JUPAS scale.

cuhk_enhanced.description: Alias for jupas_2025_enhanced. CUHK uses this for most programmes except Medicine (JS4501/JS4502 which use standard 7pt).
used_by: CUHK (most programmes, excluding JS4501 and JS4502)

polyu_weighted.description: PolyU uses the enhanced 8.5-point scale but applies large multiplier weightings (e.g. 10x-50x) to subject scores, resulting in admission scores in the 100-350 range. The base conversion is identical to jupas_2025_enhanced.
polyu_weighted.note: PolyU scores are NOT directly comparable to other universities due to large multiplier weightings
